observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that management:
Enjoin the officials of implementing offices to evaluate the status of funds and fast track the implementation and completion of the intended projects/programs, and draw the proposals to utilize funds in accordance with pertinent regulations and/or the Memorandum of Agreement with respective source agencies.
Return to the source agency the residual amounts of completed projects.
c)   The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to implement all downloaded funds in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Act.

2.9 We recommended that management be more aggressive and efficient in the full implementation of development projects to ensure that the benefits derived from these shall be enjoyed by its constituents.  If the program/projects are determined to be “not implementable”, the corresponding funds thereof should  be reprogrammed by the Municipal Development Council.

3.2 We recommended that management  facilitate the early occupancy of the stalls of the new public market building to generate more income to the municipality, and the local sanggunian  review the scheme in the payment of goodwill that will invite possible investors. On the business tax delinquency, the market administrator should send at least three (3) Demand Letters to concern businessman and have it be received by them, if no avail, apply the sanctions provided for in the market code of the Municipality to effect collection. Intensify collection of real property taxes and strictly comply with deadlines set by regulations, otherwise the Municipal Treasurer is held liable for the under collection of fines and penalties.

5.6 We recommended that management comply strictly with Civil Service regulations on attendance of its officials and employees. Immediate installation of the Biometric machines is enjoined to register the employee’s time in and out from their respective offices to ensure accuracy of their time in and out is required.

7.6 We reiterated our recommendation that management:
Stop the practice of using trust funds for payment of regular expenditures to avoid the incurrence of cash overdraft. Likewise, prompt remittance of trust funds due to other government agencies should be undertaken to avoid misappropriation thereof.
Limit the incurrence of obligations to the extent of cash availability.
Set aside in the 2017 annual budget mandatory reserve in order that the amounts utilized for other purposes out of trust funds shall be returned.
Regulate the hiring of job orders/casuals/contractual and the granting of financial assistance and austerity measures must be adopted by the LGU.

9.7 We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees. It may also as one of the  internal control measures that LGU’s may adopt to promote good governance at the local level.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a)  strictly deposit all collections daily and intact and prepare the required reports thereon;
b) closely monitor the daily collections of collectors and demand the immediate remittances of their collections in order to prevent misappropriation; and
c)  relieve from collecting functions erring collecting officers.  Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

12.11 We recommended that management  stop allocating fund for the administrative/operating expenses under the 20% development fund. The practice of appropriating part of the 20% Development Fund and other Local Funds to members of sangguniang bayan for financial assistance/priority development fund to their constituents should be discontinued. The AIP should clearly specify the name, location and amount of project, program or activity that will be funded out of the 20% Development Fund.  In addition, strictly adhere to the provisions of Republic Act 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Act and COA Circular 2012-001.

13.5 We recommended that the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate and determine all properties issued to the barangays and other national agencies and cause the turnover of the same, to serve as a basis in the dropping thereof from the books of accounts.

14.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the required Certified List of Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Assessment Roll with the corresponding tax due and collectible.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant  analyze the RPT/SET Receivable balances and make the appropriate adjustments in the books to take up the correct RPT/SET receivables during the applicable year using the Certified List of Taxpayers to be submitted to the Municipal Treasurer.  The listing of RPT delinquencies should be also reviewed to come up with a reliable amount at least equal to the RPT/SET Receivables.

15.7 We recommended that the management:
Evaluate and review the existing fund balances in order to determine the proper disposition of these dormant trust collections/cash advances; and
Coordinate with the source agencies for proper disposition thereof such as realignment of these funds for other meaningful projects.

16.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the  LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2012-002 dated September12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.

19.4 We recommended that the management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009 001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

21.7 We reiterated our recommendation that management require the concerned contractors of the completed projects to post the required warranty security to cover for any defects or damages that may occur during the warranty period before releasing the retention money.

25.8 We reiterated our recommendation that management :1)Identify and allocate an area to serve as a central storage for supplies to be carried in stock;2)In the meantime that no central storeroom is available, require all heads of departments to designate a person within their department to act as supplies custodian who will account for all receipts and issuances using the NGAS forms prescribed for Local Government Units;3)Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain supplies ledger cards;4)Require the Accounting Office to observe perpetual inventory system in the evaluation of inventories;5)Require the Inventory Team to conduct periodic inventory of supplies and render a report especially at year end and set up inventory account;6)  Ensure that the year-end balance of the inventory accounts corresponds to the value of supplies items, actually on hand at year end; and 7)Establish control procedures to regulate the issuances of supplies to actual requirements of the end users within a month.

27.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  record in the books of the municipality the cost of two (2) units tractor and mini warehouse  as part of the Property, Plant and Equipment and the corresponding Loans Payable account in the books of the local government unit.  Consistent thereto, properly include in the annual budget provisions for amortization as per Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that management facilitate the early occupation of the stalls of the new public market building to generate more income to the municipality.    The LGU may also invite investors outside the Province to make the business activity more competitive. On the business tax delinquency, the market administrator should send at least three (3) Demand Letters to concern businessman and have it be received by them, if no avail, apply the sanctions provided for in the market code of the Municipality to effect collection.
observations.managementAction:
Advertised the vacant stalls at the 2nd floor of the new market buildings thru media and posters in a tarpaulin .
As to delinquent  stall holders , the market supervisor sent notices  and   and closure orders.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management formulate policies and guidelines to be strictly adopted in the operations of the Economic Enterprise Units so as to enable the unit to generate income which shall be used to cover all the necessary expenses and make it a real self-liquidating enterprise. Otherwise, the units which fail to meet its targeted revenues and remain dependent on subsidies to cover its operations be dissolved.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still failed to reclassify all the  accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management adopt stricter measures/controls in the management of the 20% Development fund so as to successfully attain its goals and objectives. In addition, prioritize the transfer of the amount of P7.87M to the depository account of the 20% Development Fund to ensure implementation of the lined-up projects as listed in the approved AIP.

Limit the incurrence of obligations to the extent of cash availability.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that officials and employees of the municipal government with outstanding cash advances be directed to immediately liquidate/settle their cash advances to avoid the possibility of being charged due to misuse of government funds. If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations. Henceforth, monitor closely all outstanding cash advances and demand immediately the liquidations thereof. Management should strictly enforce the time limits in the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Partial liquidations were made during the year.  Some executed deed undertaking for the monthly payment of unsettled disallowances. The granting of additional cash advances was with  limitation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management expedite the titling of the sanitary landfill in the name of the Municipal Government of Maddela, copy furnished the COA through the Audit Team assigned in the LGU of all the TCTs.
observations.managementAction: Titling of the Sanitary Landfill is on going.  The MENRO/staff  will follow up its progress.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Accounting Office and the General Services Office/Property Officer reconcile the amount per actual count and the recorded balances in the general ledgers to arrive at a more accurate inventory report and establish the existence of all properties. We also recommended that management strictly adhere to the guidelines provided for under the Manual on Property Custodianship and the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is on the process of updating  property ledger cards for reconciliation with subsidiary/general ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive inform this Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project and before the start of the project/program/activities that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted. We further recommended that all infrastructure project be provided with tarpaulin signboard, adopting all the data and specifications required in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004, to inform the public of the essential feature/elements of the project to promote transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Billboards  were  placed to projects  bidded and awarded.

observations.recommendation:

2. Impose liquidated damages for the undue delay of procured goods/services. 
3. Rescind the contract for failure of the winning bidder to deliver the equipment
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages were collected to suppliers on their  final billing. The BAC see to it that the awarded projects were delivered as stipulated in the Notice of Award and the contract duration.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the significant balance of the Accounts Payable of two years old or over and which no actual claim has been filed. observations.recommendation: We again recommended that management; A) Formulate a GAD Plan with corresponding appropriation  in the annual budget designed to address gender issues within the Municipality and its constituents; B) Budget at least five (5) percent of the Municipality’s total appropriation for the GAD;  C.) Implement the GAD Plan, which shall be integrated in the regular activities of the LGU and prepare accomplishment reports for submission to agencies concerned; and D) Designate GAD Focal person to prepare and monitor reports thereon.
observations.managementAction: Reports on GAD utilization were submitted and validated.  GAD Plan was likewise prepared .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enforce settlement of all audit suspensions from the persons responsible, and disallowances from the persons deemed liable, thru cash refund, to ensure recovery of the funds of the LGU that were disbursed irregularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Accountable Officers and the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers and the official receipts promptly so that the propriety, regularity and legality of the transactions are determined on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management remit all taxes due BIR  within ten (10) days following the end of the month when the withholding was made  as required by Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 to  avoid the imposition of surcharges, interest and compromise.
observations.managementAction: Remittances to BIR were made  within ten days  following the end of the month to avoid the incurrence of penalties.
